customManagers.description: Bump tool versions pinned in Containerfiles/Dockerfiles via a `# renovate: datasource=… depName=… [versioning=…] [extractVersion=…]` comment directly above an `ARG/ENV …_VERSION=…` line. Renovate's built-in customManagers:dockerfileVersions preset only matches files literally named Dockerfile and does not capture extractVersion; the metio dev shells use `dev/Containerfile` and one marker needs extractVersion (biome), so define it here. This is what makes every hand-pinned dev/CI tool (helm, kube-score, kubeconform, cosign, helm-schema, typos, …) Renovate-owned instead of a manual bump.

Bump tool versions pinned in a GitHub Actions workflow `env:` block via a `# renovate: datasource=… depName=… [versioning=…] [extractVersion=…]` comment directly above a `…_VERSION: …` key. Mirrors customManagers:githubActionsVersions but also captures extractVersion (biome). Keeps the `curl …/releases/download/<ver>/` tool installs in verify/release workflows current without manual edits.

packageRules.description: Group updates per language ecosystem so each lands as a single PR.
Group tooling updates the same way.

Pin base-image digests (docker:pinDigests) but batch them into ONE weekly PR instead of one per upstream rebuild — trusted bases like distroless rebuild constantly, so per-rebuild PRs would be noise. The weekly window paces them; the 3-day soak is relaxed here because trusted-base digests don't get yanked, the cadence is the control.

First-party metio artifacts carry no supply-chain soak: we publish them ourselves, so the minimumReleaseAge that protects against yanked/compromised third-party releases adds no safety and only delays our own release cascade (a controller image must bump the chart's appVersion, which gates the chart release, which gates the e2e smoke tests). This covers two naming forms: the ghcr.io/metio/* images and OCI charts (docker datasource), AND the github-releases entries the helm-charts repo tracks for chart appVersions (depName metio/<repo>, no ghcr.io prefix) — the latter is deliberate because the controller release pushes the image before it creates the git tag, so the chart bump follows the tag. Overriding to 0 days lets a freshly published metio dependency update — and automerge — on the next run.

The shared metio-hugo-theme is vendored as a git submodule in the website repos. Like the other first-party metio artifacts above it is published by us, so the global 3-day minimumReleaseAge adds no supply-chain safety and only delays the theme rollout. The git-submodules manager keys on the submodule's repository URL (https://github.com/metio/metio-hugo-theme.git), which the metio/** glob above cannot match, so override it here. Every git submodule across the metio repos is this first-party theme.
